name: Barbell Bench Press
description: The king of chest exercises. Lie on a flat bench, grip the bar slightly wider than shoulder-width, lower to chest and press up explosively.
instructions: Lie flat on the bench with feet on the floor
Grip the bar just wider than shoulder-width
Unrack the bar and lower it to mid-chest
Press the bar back up explosively
Keep your back slightly arched and shoulder blades retracted

name: Incline Dumbbell Press
description: Targets the upper chest. Set bench to 30-45°, press dumbbells from shoulder level to full extension.
instructions: Set bench to 30-45 degree incline
Hold dumbbells at shoulder level with palms facing forward
Press dumbbells up and slightly inward
Lower with control to starting position
Feel the stretch at the bottom of each rep

name: Cable Flyes
description: Isolation exercise for chest. Standing cable flyes keep constant tension throughout the movement.
instructions: Set cables to upper position
Stand in the middle with one foot forward
Bring handles together in a wide arc
Hold the squeeze for 1 second
Return slowly to starting position

name: Pull-ups
description: The ultimate back width builder. Use overhand grip, pull chest to bar, lower fully.
instructions: Hang from bar with overhand grip, wider than shoulders
Retract shoulder blades before pulling
Pull chest up to the bar
Lower fully, feel the lats stretch
Avoid swinging — control the movement

name: Barbell Deadlift
description: The most powerful compound movement. Works the entire posterior chain.
instructions: Stand with bar over mid-foot
Hinge at hips, grip just outside legs
Chest up, back neutral — big breath
Drive through the floor, extend hips at top
Hinge back and lower bar under control

name: Bent-Over Barbell Row
description: Builds back thickness. Hinge forward, pull bar to lower chest/upper abs.
instructions: Stand with slight knee bend, hinge forward 45°
Overhand grip, arms straight
Pull bar to lower chest, driving elbows back
Squeeze shoulder blades together at top
Lower with control

name: Lat Pulldown
description: Machine-based pull-up alternative. Great for building lat width with controlled load.
instructions: Sit with thighs under pads, reach up for bar
Pull bar down to upper chest, elbows pointing down
Lean back slightly as you pull
Control the ascent — don't let weight jerk you up
Full stretch at the top

name: Barbell Back Squat
description: The foundational leg exercise. Bar on traps, squat below parallel, drive back up.
instructions: Bar across upper traps, grip firmly
Feet shoulder-width, toes slightly out
Brace core, big breath, break at hips and knees
Descend below parallel
Drive through heels to stand

name: Romanian Deadlift
description: Hip-hinge movement targeting hamstrings and glutes with constant tension.
instructions: Hold bar at hip level with overhand grip
Push hips back and lower bar along legs
Feel hamstring stretch at the bottom
Drive hips forward to return to start
Maintain neutral spine throughout

name: Leg Press
description: Compound leg press machine. Safer alternative to squats for high volume.
instructions: Adjust seat so knees are at 90°
Feet shoulder-width, mid-platform
Press platform away until legs are nearly straight
Slow descent, knees over toes
Don't lock knees at top

name: Walking Lunges
description: Dynamic single-leg exercise for quad and glute development and balance.
instructions: Hold dumbbells at sides or bar on traps
Step forward and lower back knee toward floor
Push off front foot to step through
Alternate legs for prescribed reps
Keep torso upright throughout

name: Overhead Press
description: Builds shoulder size and strength. Press bar from shoulder rack to full overhead extension.
instructions: Grip bar just outside shoulders
Unrack at chin level, feet hip-width
Press bar straight overhead, move head back
Lock out arms fully
Lower to chin with control

name: Dumbbell Lateral Raises
description: Isolation for lateral deltoids — key for shoulder width.
instructions: Stand with dumbbells at sides, slight elbow bend
Raise both arms to side up to shoulder height
Lead with elbows, not wrists
Pause at top, lower slowly
Lean slightly forward for more lateral delt activation

name: Face Pulls
description: Cable exercise for rear delts and rotator cuff health. Essential for shoulder longevity.
instructions: Set cable to upper pulley with rope attachment
Pull rope toward face, splitting the rope at cheek level
External rotate at peak — wrists behind elbows
Hold for 1 second
Return slowly

name: Barbell Bicep Curl
description: Classic bicep builder. Straight bar or EZ-bar curl for maximum mass.
instructions: Stand with underhand grip, arms straight
Curl bar up keeping elbows at sides
Squeeze biceps at top
Lower slowly to full extension
Don't swing — control the tempo

name: Skull Crushers
description: Tricep isolation using EZ-bar. Lie on bench, lower bar to forehead, extend.
instructions: Lie on flat bench, EZ-bar arms extended
Lower bar toward forehead by bending only elbows
Feel the tricep stretch at the bottom
Extend arms back to start
Keep upper arms vertical throughout

name: Tricep Rope Pushdown
description: Cable isolation for the tricep. Use rope attachment for full extension and spread.
instructions: Stand at high cable, grab rope with both hands
Elbows at sides, upper arms still
Push rope down until arms are fully extended
Spread the rope at the bottom for full contraction
Control the return

name: Plank
description: The foundation of core stability. Hold a straight-line position from head to heels.
instructions: Forearms on floor, elbows under shoulders
Body in straight line from head to heels
Brace core — squeeze glutes
Don't let hips sag or pike
Breathe steadily throughout

name: Cable Crunches
description: Weighted ab exercise using cable machine for progressive overload on core.
instructions: Kneel in front of cable, hold rope behind head
Hinge at hips only slightly
Crunch down by rounding the spine
Touch elbows toward knees
Slowly return to upright

name: Hanging Leg Raises
description: Difficult core exercise targeting lower abs and hip flexors while hanging from a bar.
instructions: Hang from pull-up bar, arms extended
Raise legs to 90° or higher
Avoid swinging — control the movement
Lower slowly to full hang
For easier variation: bent knee raises

name: Push-ups
description: Fundamental bodyweight chest and tricep exercise. Infinitely scalable.
instructions: Hands slightly wider than shoulders, fingers forward
Body in straight plank position
Lower chest to just above floor
Push through palms to start
Squeeze chest and triceps at top

name: Dips
description: Compound bodyweight/weighted exercise for chest and triceps.
instructions: Grip parallel bars, arms extended
Lean forward slightly for chest emphasis
Lower until elbows are at 90°
Push back up without locking elbows
Add weight belt for progression

name: Barbell Hip Thrust
description: The best glute activation exercise. Bar across hips, drive up with glutes.
instructions: Sit against bench, bar across lap over pad
Feet hip-width, toes slightly out
Drive hips up by squeezing glutes
Chin tucked, ribs down
Hold at the top for 1 second

name: Calf Raises
description: Isolation exercise for gastrocnemius and soleus. Use full range of motion.
instructions: On calf raise machine or step, balls of feet
Lower heels below platform level
Drive up onto toes, full extension
Hold at top 1-2 seconds
Lower slowly for full stretch

name: Treadmill Running
description: Classic cardio. Steady-state or interval running for cardiovascular fitness.
instructions: Start with 5-minute warm-up walk
Increase to target pace
Maintain upright posture, relaxed arms
Land midfoot, not heel striking
Cool down with 5-minute walk

name: Burpees
description: Full-body cardio and conditioning exercise. High intensity, zero equipment.
instructions: Start standing, drop to squat and place hands
Jump feet back to push-up position
Optional push-up
Jump feet toward hands
Jump up with arms overhead

name: Jump Rope
description: Excellent cardio and coordination drill used in boxing and MMA training.
instructions: Hold rope handles loosely
Jump just high enough to clear rope
Land softly on balls of feet
Keep elbows close to sides, wrists rotating
Build up to double-unders

name: Dumbbell Row
description: Single-arm dumbbell row for back thickness. Supported on bench for stability.
instructions: Place one hand and knee on bench for support
Other hand holds dumbbell straight down
Pull dumbbell to hip, driving elbow up and back
Squeeze lat at the top
Lower slowly to full extension

name: Box Jumps
description: Plyometric exercise for explosive power in the legs. Great for athletes.
instructions: Stand facing box, feet hip-width
Swing arms and bend knees to load
Explode up, pulling knees to chest
Land softly with bent knees
Step down, don't jump down

name: Kettlebell Swings
description: Hip hinge power movement. Excellent for posterior chain and conditioning.
instructions: Stand with KB on floor, hike it back
Snap hips forward powerfully
Let KB float to chest height
Hinge and hike back on descent
Keep back flat, core braced throughout
